name: Kindergarten Science: Inquiry and Evidence — Semester 1
description: A rich Kindergarten science pathway for weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning. Learners investigate phenomena through observation, data, models, engineering design, and evidence-based explanation.
learningGoals: Investigate weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning through questions, observations, measurements, and data.
Use models, patterns, and scientific reasoning to explain or predict phenomena.
Apply safe, ethical scientific and engineering practices.
Communicate a claim with evidence, reasoning, limitations, and a next question.

lessons.title: Phenomenon launch
lessons.objective: I can observe a phenomenon, record relevant details, and frame a question while studying weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.

lessons.title: Evidence lab
lessons.objective: I can collect and organize evidence through a safe investigation or data activity while studying weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.

lessons.title: Model and explanation studio
lessons.objective: I can build, test, or revise a model, explanation, or design solution while studying weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.

units.title: Observe a phenomenon: weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning
units.essentialQuestion: What is happening, and what evidence can we gather?
outcomes: Make careful observations, identify patterns, and ask testable questions.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
standardIds: NGSS: K-2 science and engineering practices
NGSS: Crosscutting concepts and engineering design

lessons.title: Phenomenon launch
lessons.objective: I can observe a phenomenon, record relevant details, and frame a question while studying weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.

activities.title: Notice and question
activities.learnerAction: With adult guidance, the learner will observe a phenomenon, record relevant details, and frame a question. Follow laboratory, outdoor, tool, material, and digital-data safety directions; record observations honestly, including results that differ from predictions.

successCriteria: I use an observation, measurement, data point, or model detail as evidence.
I explain a pattern, cause, system, or design choice with age-appropriate scientific vocabulary.
I identify a limitation, safety consideration, or next question for the investigation.

units.title: Investigate fairly: weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning
units.essentialQuestion: How can we collect trustworthy evidence?
outcomes: Plan a safe investigation, measure or record observations, and consider variables or repeated trials when developmentally appropriate.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
standardIds: NGSS: K-2 science and engineering practices
NGSS: Crosscutting concepts and engineering design

units.title: Model and design: weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning
units.essentialQuestion: How can models or designed solutions help us explain or improve a system?
outcomes: Use a diagram, physical model, data display, or prototype to explain, predict, or test a solution.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
standardIds: NGSS: K-2 science and engineering practices
NGSS: Crosscutting concepts and engineering design

units.title: Explain and communicate: weather, plants, animals, materials, and careful observation. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning
units.essentialQuestion: What claim can our evidence support, and what are its limits?
outcomes: Construct an evidence-based explanation, revise after feedback, and communicate uncertainty, next steps, or tradeoffs.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
